author: haiku-cycle-rejection <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Shipped Property 7 `add_dep_edge_never_closes_cycle` in
`crates/jjf-storage/tests/proptest_harness.rs:826-927`, plus
the helpers `ExtraEdge` / `extra_edge_strategy` /
`blocking_graph_has_cycle` / `snapshot_dep_graph` at
`crates/jjf-storage/tests/proptest_harness.rs:638-784`.

Spine: base DAG from `multi_issue_plan_strategy(1..=4, 4)`,
then ONE extra `add_dep_edge` attempt sampled 90% additive
(arbitrary owner/target/kind triple — most are cycle-closing
when the indices reverse a base edge, others are no-ops or
no-cycle additions) and 10% self-edge (exercises the
`SelfDependency` reject path on the same property). Walker is
a 3-color (white/gray/black) iterative DFS re-derived from the
post-state via `list_ids` + `read` — no incremental cache
worries, and the test is the independent oracle (doesn't reach
into `find_blocking_cycle`).

**Contract surprise:** ticket body says "typed
`Error::Invalid`" but the actual storage contract is
`Error::DependencyCycle` (see `lib.rs:2826-2833` and the
`Error` variant at `lib.rs:365`). Property matches the real
contract; documented inline.

Runs:
- Default 16 cases: 14.5s for the new property alone; full
  harness file 28.4s (was ~25s; +3.4s for Property 7).
- `PROPTEST_CASES=256` stress: 197s, green.
- `cargo nextest run --workspace`: 625 passed.

## Recommendation

Close. Acceptance criteria are met: property ships green at
16 cases, stress-runs clean at 256, failed attempts surface
typed (`DependencyCycle` / `SelfDependency`) and the pre/post
snapshot equality check confirms atomic-on-reject. The
stretch-goal "concurrent two-writer" variant is correctly out
of scope (would need a custom harness on top of
`add_dep_edge`); file as a follow-up only if a real bug shows
up in that window.

## Confidence

high. Property fires the cycle-reject path frequently (every
back-edge against the DAG triggers it; SelfDep branch fires
~10% of cases), the walker is independently derived from the
spec definition of "blocking graph", and 256-case stress is
clean. The contract correction (`Error::DependencyCycle` vs
`Error::Invalid`) is a documentation-only nit on the ticket
body — the storage code is the source of truth.

## Open follow-ups

- none.
